title: agent-core: tool system gaps - no AbortSignal, no streaming, missing metadata
issueBody:
## Summary
Tool execution system missing several capabilities:

- No shared AbortSignal for long-running tools (each must own timeout)
- No tool output streaming (onUpdate callback exists but unused)
- Missing tool metadata: category/tags, cost hints, rateLimit, requiresApproval
- No error types (all errors become text strings)
- SSH tool incomplete
- No caching of tool results, web fetches, or schema fetches
- Hardcoded limits (50MB output, 120s bash, 30s search) not configurable

Ref: `docs/agent-layer-gaps.md`

issueComments.author: tmuskal
issueComments.body:
**Related:** #576 (tool registry - metadata, AbortSignal belong in unified registry)
**Related:** #587 (abort fix enables tool cancellation via AbortSignal)

issueComments.author: a5c-ai
issueComments.body: Triage says this is a valid umbrella issue for unifying and hardening the agent tool execution system across agent-core, tool-mux, and agent-platform. Recommended approach: define the unified tool contract first; add contract tests before implementation for built-in tools, custom/plugin tools, tool_search/tool_fetch, tool-mux dispatch, and MCP-backed tools; wire agent-core execution through shared tool-mux dispatcher/registry; implement slices for cancellation, typed results/errors, streaming/onUpdate, metadata/policy enforcement, SSH behavior, caching, and configurable limits. Keep cache behavior opt-in for read-only tools until side-effect metadata, cache keys, TTLs, and invalidation are explicit.

relatedIssues.summary: tool registry - metadata and AbortSignal belong in unified registry
abort fix enables tool cancellation via AbortSignal

planningNotes: Use flat phase list because the gap categories and affected packages are known.
Perform Phase 0 reuse audit before proposing new infrastructure.
Treat DeferredToolRegistry as a migration surface, not the long-term target architecture.
Keep cache behavior opt-in and limited to read-only tools until side-effect metadata and invalidation semantics are explicit.
Use sparse breakpoints: one after low-confidence runtime trace if needed, one mandatory unified contract review before implementation, one only if final gates fail.

implementationSlices.title: shared execution context, AbortSignal, streaming event, ToolResult, ToolError, and metadata types
agent-core execution through tool-mux registry/dispatcher and discovery metadata flow
built-in tool cancellation, streaming updates, typed errors, and configurable timeout/output limits
focus: configured timeout and output limit plumbing

implementationSlices.title: metadata-driven policy, approval/rate/cost hints, SSH hardening, and opt-in read-only caching
focus: SSH host-key policy and keepalive/retry controls
